toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-c4101dd4bfba4280a083bd1a2f25ef93 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1
arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` with crash dump collection. For .NET, crash dumps can be collected using environment variables that configure the .NET runtime's crash dump behavior. The key environment variables are:

- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables crash dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — sets the dump type (4 = Full dump, which is most useful; 1=Normal, 2=WithHeap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — sets the output path for the dump file


- These variables replaced the older `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump` format in .NET 6+

Now I'll set up the environment variables and run the tests with a reasonable dump output path.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-c4101dd4bfba4280a083bd1a2f25ef93 && \
  mkdir -p CrashDumps && \
  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName="$(pwd)/CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t" \
  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1
arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's how crash dump collection was configured:

| Environment Variable | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` | `1` | Enables crash dump on unhandled exception/SIGSEGV |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType` | `4` | Full dump (includes all memory) |
| `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` | `CrashDumps/coredump.%p.%t` | Dump path with PID and timestamp placeholders |

No crashes occurred, so the `CrashDumps/` directory is empty. If a test had crashed the host process, a full core dump would have been written there automatically by the .NET runtime.
